description: Cross-language bridge links (Tauri commands, NAPI exports, PyO3 functions, FFI, HTTP routes, etc.). Call before modifying any IPC or FFI boundary so you see every cross-language call site.

file_path.description: Filter links where either endpoint is in this file path

project.description: Optional linked-project label. Omit for primary corpus.

query.description: Search query to filter by binding key or symbol name (case-insensitive substring match)

name: ministr_bridge

description: Clone a git repository and index its content. Supports sparse checkout. Cached clones are reused.

branch.description: Optional branch to clone (defaults to repository default)

paths.description: Optional paths for sparse checkout (e.g. ['docs', 'src']). Omit for full checkout.

repo.description: Remote git repository URL to clone (e.g. 'https://github.com/owner/repo.git')

name: ministr_clone

description: Extractive TF-IDF summaries (roughly 60-80% shorter) for sections you want to keep referenceable without their full text. Pair with ministr_dropped after dropping the originals.

content_ids.description: Content IDs (section IDs) to generate compressed summaries for

project.description: Optional linked-project label. Omit for primary corpus.

name: ministr_compress

description: Find symbols with zero references — candidates for safe deletion. Filters out `pub` symbols, entry points, and trivial helpers. Double-check with `ministr_references` before deleting since dynamic dispatch isn't tracked.

limit.description: Maximum results to return. Default 50, capped at 500.

min_lines.description: Skip symbols whose body is shorter than this many lines. Default 1.

project.description: Optional linked-project label. Omit for primary corpus.

name: ministr_dead

description: Full source of a code symbol by ID. Call ministr_references first if you intend to modify or delete the symbol. Pass blame=true for git authorship of the symbol's lines.

blame.description: When true, attach git blame metadata (per-author line counts + last commit) for the symbol's line range. Omitted when not a git repo.

col.description: 0-based byte column for position-addressed lookup (requires file+line)

file.description: Position-addressed alternative to symbol_id: file path (with line+col) to resolve the symbol under the cursor

project.description: Optional linked-project label. Omit for primary corpus.

symbol_id.description: Symbol ID to get the definition for (from ministr_symbols results)

name: ministr_definition

description: Run the project's own toolchain(s) (cargo/tsc/eslint/ruff/go vet/…, plus any SARIF-emitting tool) and return bounded STRUCTURED diagnostics (file, range, severity, code, message), errors first, each cross-linked to the enclosing symbol. The agentic verify step — structured compiler/lint feedback as data, never raw build logs. Language-agnostic.

languages.description: Restrict to these toolchain languages (e.g. 'rust','typescript','python','go'). Omit to run every detected toolchain.

limit.description: Maximum diagnostics to return. Default 100, capped at 500.

project.description: Optional linked-project label. Omit for primary corpus.

name: ministr_diagnostics

description: Call immediately after dropping content you previously received. Keeps ministr's view of what you still have accurate; without this, future ministr_read calls on dropped IDs return short 'already delivered' stubs instead of the full text.

content_ids.description: Content IDs the agent has dropped from its context

name: ministr_dropped

description: Atomic claims from a section, optionally query-filtered. Cheaper than ministr_read when you don't need full prose.

project.description: Optional linked-project label. Omit for primary corpus.

query.description: Optional query to filter claims by relevance

section_id.description: Section ID to extract claims from

name: ministr_extract

description: Fetch a URL from the web and index its content. Tries llms.txt first, falls back to direct page fetch.

depth.description: Crawl depth for following links (default: 0 = single page only)

max_pages.description: Maximum number of pages to fetch when crawling (default: 50)

path_filter.description: Only fetch URLs whose path starts with this prefix (e.g. '/docs/')

name: ministr_fetch

description: Transitive blast radius of changing a symbol. Returns every caller / implementor / importer N levels deep, plus distinct files, distinct test files, and a low/medium/high risk score. Use BEFORE recommending a non-trivial refactor.

direction.description: Call-graph direction: 'incoming' = transitive callers / blast radius (default), 'outgoing' = transitive callees (what this symbol calls).

max_depth.description: Maximum BFS depth to walk the call graph. Default 3, capped at 10.

project.description: Optional linked-project label. Omit for primary corpus.

range.description: Git revision range (e.g. 'main..HEAD', 'HEAD~3'). When set, analyzes the diff-aware blast radius: which indexed symbols the range touched and the union of what they can break. Overrides symbol_id.

repo_path.description: Path inside the git work tree to resolve `range` against. Defaults to the server's working directory. Only used with `range`.

symbol_id.description: Symbol ID whose blast radius should be analyzed (from ministr_symbols results)

tests_only.description: When true, keep only nodes in test files. With direction 'incoming' this answers 'which tests transitively exercise this symbol' (the minimal test set for a change).

name: ministr_impact

description: List the current project plus any linked projects you can query in this session. Pass a returned `label` as the `project` argument to other tools; omit `project` for the current one. Linked projects come from `.ministr.toml` `[[linked]]` entries (or `ministr_clone` results).
name: ministr_projects

description: Full content of a section by ID. Call ministr_extract instead if you only need atomic claims.

project.description: Optional linked-project label. Omit for primary corpus.

name: ministr_read

description: All callers, implementors, and importers of a code symbol. Call before deleting or significantly modifying any non-trivial public symbol — zero references means safe to delete.

col.description: 0-based byte column for position-addressed lookup (requires file+line)

file.description: Position-addressed alternative to symbol_id: file path (with line+col) to resolve the symbol under the cursor

limit.description: Maximum number of entries to return (default: 100)

project.description: Optional linked-project label. Omit for primary corpus.

symbol_id.description: Symbol ID to find references for (from ministr_symbols results)

through_implementors.description: When true, also include callers of the same-named method on co-implementor types (LSP references-including-overrides / type hierarchy). Bounded; combine with ref_kind 'calls' or omit ref_kind.

name: ministr_references

description: Check cached web and git sources for staleness and re-fetch changed content.

url.description: Optional URL (web) or repo URL (git) to check for staleness. If omitted, checks all cached sources.

name: ministr_refresh

description: Follow relationship edges (references, contradicts, depends_on, updates) from a claim. Use when one claim's truth depends on another.

claim_id.description: Claim ID to find related claims for

project.description: Optional linked-project label. Omit for primary corpus.

name: ministr_related

description: Run a shell command (recorded + captured). Returns exit code + a token-lean digest with every error line; full log via ministr_run_logs. background:true returns a run_id immediately.

background.description: Run in background; poll with ministr_run_status

cwd.description: Working directory; defaults to the first corpus root

name: ministr_run

description: Cancel a running run; kills the whole process group.
name: ministr_run_kill

description: Page a run's captured log (delta: only what you haven't seen) or filter it with query=substring.

from_offset.description: Explicit byte offset (overrides the session cursor)

query.description: Substring filter: return matching lines instead of paging

name: ministr_run_logs

description: Poll a run's status (running/exited/killed/timed_out, exit code, duration, bytes).
name: ministr_run_status

description: Detect possible SOLID-principle violations across the codebase deterministically. Returns clusters / findings labelled by principle (dry_ocp, srp, isp, dip). Filter by kind/module and toggle principles via params. Pair with ministr_references before refactoring.

container_kinds.description: Override container kinds for SRP detection. Defaults: ['impl','struct','class','mod']

cyclic_min_edges_per_direction.description: CyclicDependency: minimum distinct cross-package edges per direction. Single-edge cycles are usually phantom name-resolution artefacts. Default 2.

cyclic_skip_test_paths.description: CyclicDependency: skip edges touching test/fixture paths. Sample data shouldn't drive the workspace dependency graph. Default true.

interface_kinds.description: Override interface kinds for ISP/DIP detection. Defaults: ['trait','interface','protocol']

isp_max_overlap_fraction.description: Implementor under-using cutoff (fraction of trait methods overlapped). Default 0.33.

isp_min_methods.description: Minimum interface method count before ISP fires. Default 6.

jaccard_threshold.description: Jaccard threshold over callee-sets for DRY/OCP. Default 0.4.

limit.description: Maximum findings to return. Default 50, capped at 500.

max_pairs.description: Hard cap on pairwise comparisons inside any DRY/OCP bucket. Default 100k.

min_lines.description: Skip candidate symbols shorter than this many lines. Default 5.

principles.description: Principles to evaluate: 'dry_ocp', 'srp', 'isp', 'dip', 'shotgun_surgery', 'cyclic_dependency'. Omit or pass empty to run all six.

project.description: Optional linked-project label. Omit for primary corpus.

representative_count.description: Maximum representative members per component list. Larger arrays are truncated and reported via `*_omitted`. Default 5.

shotgun_max_jaccard.description: Maximum callee-set Jaccard for ShotgunSurgery. Above this the group is treated as a Type-4 clone and handled by 'dry_ocp'. Default 0.5.

shotgun_min_packages.description: Minimum distinct packages a Shotgun-Surgery group must span. Default 2.

shotgun_min_sites.description: Minimum file count before a Shotgun-Surgery finding fires. Default 3.

shotgun_skip_conventional_names.description: Skip Shotgun-Surgery groups whose name is universally conventional (new/default/fmt/clone/as_str/parse/main/etc.). Default true.

similarity_threshold.description: Cosine threshold for DRY/OCP clone detection. Default 0.86.

srp_cohesion_threshold.description: Cosine threshold for SRP within-container cohesion edges. Default 0.7.

name: ministr_solid

description: Search the indexed corpus by natural-language query. Start here for any vague question; follow up with ministr_read (full text) or ministr_extract (atomic claims) on top results.

corpus_boost.description: Optional per-corpus score multipliers for cross-corpus ranking. Map<corpus_id, multiplier>; absent corpora default to 1.0; clamped to [0, 10]. Use 2.0 to float your own repo above Atlas hits, 0.0 to suppress a corpus.

corpus_ids.description: Optional cross-corpus list. When set and non-empty, fans the query out across each corpus_id (own corpora or Atlas slugs), tags hits with source_corpus, and merges results by score. Omit to query a single corpus.

project.description: Optional linked-project label (from .ministr.toml [[linked]]). Omit for the session's primary corpus. Call ministr_projects to list labels.

query.description: Natural language query to search the corpus

top_k.description: Maximum number of results to return

name: ministr_survey

description: Find code symbols (functions, structs, traits, etc.) by name, kind, module, or visibility. Pair with ministr_definition for source and ministr_references before modifying.

limit.description: Maximum number of entries to return (default: 100)

module.description: Module path prefix filter (e.g. 'config' matches config::sub)

project.description: Optional linked-project label. Omit for primary corpus.

query.description: Fuzzy symbol name search (case-insensitive substring match)

name: ministr_symbols

description: Poll a background task status. Deprecated: prefer MCP tasks/get.

task_id.description: Task ID to check status for

name: ministr_task

description: Structural overview (table of contents) of the indexed corpus. Use to orient on an unfamiliar codebase before drilling in.

document_id.description: Optional document ID to filter the table of contents to a single document

limit.description: Maximum number of entries to return (default: 100)

project.description: Optional linked-project label. Omit for primary corpus.

name: ministr_toc

description: Internal ministr accounting (a rough token estimate of what it has delivered so far). Advisory only and anchored to a configured window, not your real model context window — do NOT use it to decide you are low on context or to stop work. Safe to ignore.
name: ministr_usage
